title: Today's AI Brief: OpenAI and Anthropic dig in against each other on AI jobs apocalypse - Axios
summary: A look at the AI jobs apocalypse debate between OpenAI and Anthropic
imageAlt: Image from Axios for OpenAI and Anthropic dig in against each other on AI jobs apocalypse - Axios
imageCaption: OpenAI and Anthropic dig in against each other on AI jobs apocalypse - Axios (Axios)

body.text: The AI Jobs Apocalypse: A Debate Heats Up

I'm looking at a fresh report from Axios that caught my eye - OpenAI and Anthropic are going head-to-head on the topic of AI's impact on jobs. For some background, we're in a platform shift with AI, where new models are changing how work gets done. The economics of these models are still being figured out, and data boundaries are being pushed. Safety, trust, distribution, and adoption are all up for debate.

According to Axios, OpenAI and Anthropic are staking out different positions on the AI jobs apocalypse. My read is that both are trying to shape the narrative around AI's impact on the workforce. OpenAI seems to be arguing that AI will augment human capabilities, while Anthropic is sounding the alarm on potential job displacement. It's a bit like the old 'glass half full or glass half empty' debate, but with more robots.

What stands out to me is that this debate has real-world implications. As AI models get better and more widespread, people are wondering what will happen to their jobs. Will AI create new opportunities, or will it displace workers? The answer, of course, is complicated. I think it's safe to say that AI is going to change the job market in some fundamental ways, but it's hard to predict exactly how.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
